touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn connection request sent to Elijah Bauer at MapleEngine. No response received.
Cold email sent to Elijah introducing pipeline visibility solution for revenue operations teams. No response received.
Follow-up email referencing pipeline visibility challenges for mid-market SaaS companies. Elijah responded expressing interest in learning more about forecast accuracy improvements.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to what Gong offers for pipeline visibility?
questions_asked.answer: Our platform focuses specifically on deal-level risk signals and predictive scoring, whereas Gong excels at conversation intelligence. Many teams use both together.

touches.ai_summary: Elijah replied to earlier outreach confirming current challenges with pipeline accuracy at MapleEngine. Mentioned they're evaluating a few options including Gong.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We're hoping to have something in place by end of quarter.

next_steps: Send calendar invite for qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Elijah to understand MapleEngine's revenue operations challenges. Discussed current manual forecasting process causing visibility gaps across their 13-person sales team. Elijah confirmed he's the Head of RevOps and has budget authority, though final approval needs SVP sign-off.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for a pipeline visibility solution right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our forecast accuracy has dropped below 70% the last two quarters. Leadership needs better visibility into deal health.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Isaac Ito, our SVP of Revenue, will need to approve. Also want to loop in a few sales managers and our implementation lead Fatima.

next_steps: Send deck on pipeline visibility ROI

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call diving into MapleEngine's current tech stack and forecasting workflow. Elijah walked through their pain points with manual pipeline reviews and lack of automated risk detection. Confirmed they're also talking to Gong but concerned about implementation complexity.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: For 13 seats, usually 2-3 weeks including CRM integration and team training.

questions_asked.question: What's most important to you—ease of use, depth of analytics, or speed to value?
questions_asked.answer: Speed to value. We need something our team will actually use daily, not another tool that collects dust.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation with sales manager

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Benjamin Suzuki (Sales Manager) to review platform capabilities. Meeting was rescheduled once due to conflict. Benjamin tested pipeline scoring features and asked detailed questions about CRM integration and data accuracy. Expressed concern about whether automation tier is necessary versus starting with basic plan.

questions_asked.question: Can we integrate with our custom Salesforce fields for deal scoring?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our API supports custom field mapping during setup.

questions_asked.question: What's the difference in value between the professional and unlimited automation tiers?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited gets you automated risk alerts, custom workflows, and priority support. Professional is manual triggers only.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a must-have versus nice-to-have for your team?
questions_asked.answer: If it actually predicts which deals will slip before we waste time on them. Our reps need early warning signals.

objections: Concerned about learning curve for sales team who are already stretched thin
next_steps: Benjamin to test with sample deals

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Elijah recapping technical evaluation feedback from Benjamin. Shared case study from similar-sized SaaS company that improved forecast accuracy by 28% in first quarter.

questions_asked.question: Can you send over preliminary pricing for 13 seats with unlimited automation?
questions_asked.answer: Sending formal proposal today. Standard pricing is around $13,500 ACV but we can discuss options on our call.

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Elijah to discuss proposal. Initial quote of $13,500 met with pushback due to budget constraints and Gong's competitive pricing. Negotiated discount to $9,100 ACV to match budget and close before end of quarter. Elijah committed to bringing proposal to Isaac (SVP) for executive approval.

questions_asked.question: Can you match what Gong is offering us at around $9,000?
questions_asked.answer: We can work within that range. I can approve $9,100 ACV with unlimited automation tier if we can close this month.

questions_asked.question: What's Isaac's main concern going to be when you present this?
questions_asked.answer: ROI timeline and whether we're buying the right tier. He'll want to see the business case.

objections: Budget allocated is closer to $9K, not $13.5K as initially quoted
next_steps: Schedule executive meeting with Isaac Ito

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Isaac Ito (SVP of Revenue) to review business case and ROI projections. Isaac asked pointed questions about competitive positioning versus Gong and why unlimited automation tier is recommended. Elijah championed the solution throughout. Isaac expressed concern about discount level but acknowledged need for pipeline visibility tool this quarter.

questions_asked.question: Why should we choose this over Gong when they're offering similar pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Gong is conversation intelligence first. We're purpose-built for pipeline scoring and deal risk prediction. Different use cases, complementary tools.

questions_asked.question: What's the risk if we start with a lower tier and upgrade later?
questions_asked.answer: No technical risk, but you'd miss automated alerts in the first quarter when you need visibility most. Migration is seamless though.

questions_asked.question: What would make this an easy yes for you?
questions_asked.answer: Proof it'll improve our forecast accuracy faster than Gong. Elijah trusts it, so I'm inclined to move forward if legal and procurement clear it.

objections: Significant discount raises questions about standard pricing credibility
next_steps: Isaac to loop in Madison Liu (procurement)
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Elijah post-executive meeting. Confirmed Isaac's conditional approval pending procurement and legal review. Addressed remaining questions about onboarding timeline and training for Fatima's team. Madison Liu (procurement) now engaged to finalize contract terms.

questions_asked.question: When can we start onboarding if we sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: Kickoff call within 3 business days of signature. Full deployment in 2-3 weeks with Fatima leading implementation.
